Code fragment: public final TableField<CurrencyRecord, Integer> ID = createField("ID", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'ID' column as primary key when a natural key already exists via the unique constraint on CODE (CONSTRAINT_50). Currency codes are standardized (e.g., ISO 4217), immutable, and would make a suitable primary key. Consider using CODE as the primary key instead of the synthetic ID column.

Using selectFrom(ACCOUNT) fetches all columns from the ACCOUNT table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed, such as select(ACCOUNT.ID, ACCOUNT.NAME, ...).from(ACCOUNT).

Using selectFrom(ACCOUNT) fetches all columns from the ACCOUNT table when only the ACTIVE column needs to be updated. Consider selecting only the required columns or using an update statement directly with update(ACCOUNT).set(ACCOUNT.ACTIVE, false).where(ACCOUNT.ID.eq(accountID)).

Using selectFrom(TRANSACTIONS) fetches all columns from the TRANSACTIONS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns using .select(TRANSACTIONS.COLUMN1, TRANSACTIONS.COLUMN2, ...).from(TRANSACTIONS).

Using selectFrom(CURRENCY) fetches all columns from the CURRENCY table when only CODE and FACTOR appear to be needed. Consider explicitly selecting only the required columns to improve query efficiency.
Using selectFrom(ACCOUNT) fetches all columns from the ACCOUNT table, which may include unnecessary data. Consider explicitly selecting only the columns needed (ID, ACTIVE, CURRENCY_CODE, BALANCE).
